questions.text: Which of the following is NOT matter?
questions.explanation: Matter is anything that occupies space and has mass. Hate is a feeling/emotion and has no mass or volume.

questions.text: The process of mixing different substances on their own is called:
questions.explanation: Diffusion is the intermixing of particles of two different types of matter on their own.

questions.text: What happens to the rate of diffusion as temperature increases?
questions.explanation: With increase in temperature, kinetic energy of particles increases, causing them to move faster and diffuse more quickly.

questions.text: The SI unit of mass is:
questions.explanation: The International System (SI) unit for mass is the kilogram (kg).

questions.text: Which state of matter has a definite shape and a definite volume?
questions.explanation: Solids have a fixed shape and volume due to strong intermolecular forces.

questions.text: Which of the following has the highest compressibility?
questions.explanation: Gases are highly compressible compared to solids and liquids because of large spaces between particles.

questions.text: The change of state directly from solid to gas without changing into liquid is called:
questions.explanation: Sublimation is the transition where a solid directly becomes a gas (e.g., Camphor or Dry Ice).

questions.text: Which of the following will produce severe burns?
questions.explanation: Steam at 100°C has more energy than water at the same temperature due to the latent heat of vaporisation.

questions.text: The boiling point of water on the Kelvin scale is:
questions.explanation: K = °C + 273. For boiling water, 100 + 273 = 373 K.

questions.text: Evaporation causes:
questions.explanation: Particles of liquid absorb energy from surroundings to evaporate, resulting in a cooling effect.

questions.text: Which factor does NOT increase the rate of evaporation?
questions.explanation: High humidity means air already has a lot of water vapor, which decreases the rate of further evaporation.

questions.text: What is the physical state of water at 250°C?
questions.explanation: Since the boiling point of water is 100°C, it exists as a gas at 250°C.

questions.text: Dry ice is:
questions.explanation: Solid CO2 is called dry ice because it sublimes directly into gas without melting.

questions.text: The strength of force of attraction between particles is maximum in:
questions.explanation: Solids have the strongest intermolecular forces compared to liquids and gases.

questions.text: Kinetic energy of particles is minimum in:
questions.explanation: Particles in solids are tightly packed and only vibrate at their mean positions.

questions.text: Which of the following is the SI unit of pressure?
questions.explanation: Pressure is measured in Pascals (Pa).

questions.text: Latent heat of fusion is the amount of heat energy required to change 1kg of solid into liquid at:
questions.explanation: It occurs at atmospheric pressure at the solid's melting point.

questions.text: Acetone or perfume feels cold on the palm because:
options: It absorbs energy from palm to evaporate
questions.explanation: Evaporation is an endothermic process that takes heat from the surface (the palm).

questions.text: Cotton clothes are preferred in summer because:
options: They absorb sweat and expose it to evaporation
questions.explanation: Cotton is a good absorber of water, helping sweat evaporate and cool the body.

questions.text: We can sip hot tea faster from a saucer than from a cup due to:
questions.explanation: Increased surface area increases the rate of evaporation and cooling.

questions.text: The Fourth state of matter is:
questions.explanation: Plasma consists of super energetic and super excited particles in the form of ionised gases.

questions.text: LPG stands for:
questions.explanation: LPG is Petroleum gas compressed into liquid form for easier storage and transport.

questions.text: Convert 300 K to Celsius scale.
Density is defined as:
questions.explanation: Density = Mass per unit volume.

questions.text: Fluorescent tubes and neon sign bulbs consist of:
questions.explanation: The gas inside gets ionised when electricity flows, creating plasma.

questions.text: When water boils, the temperature ________ until all water turns to steam.
questions.explanation: The temperature remains constant during change of state because heat is used as latent heat.

questions.text: Which of the following describes 'Fluidity'?
questions.explanation: Fluidity is the ability of liquids and gases to flow and change shape.

questions.text: Zig-zag movement of particles is called:
questions.explanation: Brownian motion describes the random motion of particles in a fluid.

questions.text: Ice floats on water because:
options: Its density is less than water
questions.explanation: Ice has a cage-like structure that makes its density lower than liquid water.

questions.text: Naphthalene balls disappear after some time due to:
questions.explanation: Naphthalene is a sublimable solid that turns directly into vapor.

questions.text: Which state of matter has neither a fixed shape nor a fixed volume?
questions.explanation: Gases fill the entire container and have no fixed boundaries.

questions.text: Which of the following is not a property of solids?
questions.explanation: Solids have negligible compressibility.

questions.text: Melting point is a measure of the ________ between particles.
questions.explanation: Higher the melting point, stronger the force of attraction between particles.

questions.text: Atmospheric pressure at sea level is taken as:
questions.explanation: 1 atmosphere (atm) is the normal pressure at sea level.

questions.text: Water droplets on the outer surface of a glass containing ice-cold water are due to:
questions.explanation: Water vapor in air loses energy on contact with the cold glass and turns to liquid.

questions.text: The density of air is ________ than the density of water.
questions.explanation: Gas particles are far apart, leading to much lower mass per unit volume.

questions.text: Solidification is also known as:
questions.explanation: Changing a liquid to a solid is called solidification or freezing.

questions.text: Increasing pressure and decreasing temperature can ________ gases.
questions.explanation: By bringing particles closer and slowing them down, gases can be turned into liquids.

questions.text: Evaporation is a ________ phenomenon.
questions.explanation: Evaporation only occurs at the surface of the liquid, unlike boiling.

questions.text: Boiling is a ________ phenomenon.
questions.explanation: Boiling involves the entire mass of the liquid.
